codeReview.summary: Current implementation still satisfies the lifecycle identity and close-accounting contract: a single Rust classification helper drives WindowKindInfo and user-window checks, and exit decisions only trigger after the closing label is a visible user window and the post-close visible user-window count reaches zero. The reviewed Rust regression coverage directly exercises startup, repeated opens, reopen sequences, label classification, and last-user-window exit behavior, and I did not find contract-breaking issues in the current code.

This refresh aligns the scrutiny artifact with the current implementation state being reviewed.

summary: Pass. Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton/handoff for session de6cba55-2762-47de-a9dc-77838b90e0ac, the current src-tauri/src/utils/window.rs lifecycle implementation, and fresh validator evidence from cargo test window::tests plus bun test. The current code still preserves one authoritative primary main classification, keeps support/prewarm labels out of visible user-window identity, and bases exit decisions only on visible user windows.
